graded_decisions.news_since: NVDA Q1 revenue $81.6B (+85% YoY); Q2 guidance $91B; ATH $236.54 on May 14; stock -3.85% past week (Motley Fool, 2026-05-28)
graded_decisions.comment: Dip-buy on strong AI fundamentals but NVDA remains in a downtrend from ATH. Position essentially flat. Thesis (AI infrastructure demand) intact; price momentum weak.

graded_decisions.news_since: Energy cooled in Q2; XLE -2% in Q2 after +32% YTD; Iran war creating sector uncertainty (BigGo Finance, 2026-05-22)
graded_decisions.comment: Well-timed exit. Macro thesis correct: energy sector stalled post-Q1 surge. XLE continued lower after sell. Rare clean application of regime-awareness.

graded_decisions.news_since: Microsoft Build conference May 28-29: new AI coding model for GitHub Copilot, suite of homegrown AI models; MSFT +7.6% over 2 sessions (Motley Fool, 2026-05-28); analyst PT $560
graded_decisions.comment: Sold the day before a major scheduled catalyst (Microsoft Build). No calendar check performed. MSFT +7.6% in two days post-sell. Costliest decision of the week.

graded_decisions.news_since: AAPL set ATH closing price $312.51 on May 28; iOS 27 with revamped Siri previewed; WWDC upcoming; market cap +16% in 30 days to $4.59T (MacDailyNews, 2026-05-28)
graded_decisions.comment: Entered near ATH; slightly underwater. WWDC catalyst ahead keeps thesis alive but entry chased momentum rather than waiting for a pullback.

graded_decisions.news_since: NVDA recovering from post-ATH pullback; strong Q1/Q2 guidance intact; trimmed near intraday lows (open $211.26); stock rebounded to $215.26 next session
graded_decisions.comment: Sold near the bottom of the pullback — below the May 26 buy price of $215.225. Combined with the May 26 buy this creates a churn loop: buy high, sell lower. Contradicts AI-fundamentals thesis.

graded_decisions.news_since: Tech rally driven by MSFT Build AI announcements; QQQ continued upward from entry
graded_decisions.comment: Timely entry into tech AI rally. Position benefiting from MSFT and broader growth re-rating. Hold.

graded_decisions.news_since: AAPL hit intraday ATH $315.00 today; iOS 27 AI features; Amazon taking over Apple 20% Globalstar stake; positive AI narrative (Yahoo Finance, 2026-05-29)
graded_decisions.comment: Bought at the intraday high on the day AAPL set its ATH. Closed -0.8%. WWDC catalyst still ahead; thesis intact but entry chased the very peak. Wait for next-session confirmation.

patterns: Catalyst blindspot on exits: MSFT sold the day before Microsoft Build conference, missing +7.6%. No pre-exit calendar check was performed — the single most expensive error of the week.

NVDA churn: bought 2 @ $215.225 (May 26) then sold 5 @ $212.70 (May 28), below the recent buy price, and stock recovered to $215.26. Net effect: reduced position into a dip and locked in a loss on the churn leg.
ATH accumulation without pullback: both AAPL entries ($312.46 and $313.17) chased price at or within 0.2% of all-time high. Intraday reversals left both entries underwater by close.

High US large-cap tech concentration: AAPL + NVDA + MSFT + QQQ + SPY = 93%+ of portfolio in correlated US growth. The correct XLE exit created diversification opportunity that was rotated straight back into tech.
suggested_adjustments: Catalyst calendar gate: Before exiting any position, check for scheduled events (earnings, product conferences, macro releases) in the next 7 trading days. Do not exit on a routine trim if a known catalyst is within that window unless a specific stop-loss level is hit.

Churn prevention — same-symbol cooldown: Do not sell a symbol at a price more than 0.5% below the most recent buy on that symbol within 3 trading sessions. If NVDA was bought at $215.225, do not sell below ~$214.15 within 3 sessions without a hard invalidation trigger.

ATH entry rule: Require 2+ independent news sources confirming a fresh catalyst before adding to a position within 1% of its 52-week high. Prefer entries on 1-2% intraday pullbacks rather than chasing momentum at the high-of-day.

Sector concentration cap: Limit any single sector (US large-cap tech) to 65% of portfolio market value. Currently ~93%. Redeploy rotated proceeds into an uncorrelated sector or short-duration fixed income when no high-conviction name is available.
summary: Week ending 2026-05-29: Portfolio ~+0.56% vs SPY +1.43%, VT +1.73%, SSO +2.60%. Underperformed all benchmarks; -0.87% alpha vs SPY. Primary drag: MSFT sold day-before Build conference missed +7.6% in two sessions. Secondary drag: NVDA churn (buy 215.225, sell 212.70) and ATH AAPL entries. XLE exit was correctly timed. First review — decisions journal was empty so grades are based on order fills only; session reasoning will be captured going forward. Note: SSO returned +2.60% vs theoretical 2x SPY of +2.86%; -0.26% path-dependent drag from intraweek volatility.
